content.full_text:
EMT Associates, Inc.
Evaluation, Management and Training
3090 Fite Circle, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95827
(916) 363-9415

August 20, 1990

Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Turvill:

In response to your letter dated August 16, 1990, (see enclosed copy) I submit the following answers to three questions raised in regard to the Conflict of Interest Code for the Center for Management Development:

1. EMT Associates, Inc. was incorporated in the State of California in 1981.

2. EMT Associates, Inc. was not created as a result of any contractual relationship with the Center for Management Development.

3. When EMT contracts with trainers for specific training workshops, these are exclusively available to the members and affiliates of the Center for Management Development. We develop a new roster of courses and trainers from year to year, under new contracts, based on needs expressed by the Center for Management Development.

Thank you for your inquiry. If I can provide additional information please contact me directly.

Sincerely,

Lynne P. Cannady

Lynne P. Cannady

LPC/ba

Enclosure

DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES PROVIDED BY EMT ASSOCIATES, INC.,
UNDER CONTRACT TO THE CENTER FOR MANAGEMENT DEVELOPMENT

By virtue of the authority vested in a Joint Powers Agreement (among the cities of Davis,
Fairfield, Napa, Vacaville, Woodland, Benicia, Lodi Public Works Department and the
counties of San Joaquin and Solano), the Center for Management Development contracts
with EMT Associates, Inc., to provide management training workshops for public employees.
EMT Associates has been providing this service under annual contracts since 1982.

The services rendered by EMT Associates include conducting a training needs assessment
survey at the close of each contract year to identify courses of future interests. Following
the needs assessment, we propose a roster an schedule of courses and a budget estimate of
costs to render these services. Specific tasks associated with this contract include:

1. Distribute, collect, and analyze annual training needs assessment survey data;

2. Prepare a roster of 30-35 courses to be offered between September and May;

3. Contact community facilities and trainer consultants to schedule courses, and contract
with trainers;

4. Disseminate descriptions of courses, schedule, and registration forms;

5. Log all registrations, develop roll sheets, and monitor enrollment from each
participating jurisdiction (based on number of training slots purchases in advance);

6. For each course, EMT staff prepare workshop handouts, arrange for use of audio-
visual equipment, and fulfill all logistical requirements in advance and on site at the
training location;

7. For each course, EMT staff return to training site to "break down" the set up, return
equipment, and collect evaluation summaries from course participants.

8. Submit an invoice and monthly report to CMD members and affiliates, describing
registration and attendance, and evaluation for the month.

9. Attend meetings with CMD members and affiliates as needed, usually three or four
times per contract period.

10. At the close of each year, EMT provides a cumulative summary of all registration an
attendance data, by jurisdiction, as well as final course evaluation scores.

To carry out this contract EMT relies mostly on our staff receptionist to conduct all logistical
and day to day tasks. A senior professional directs the contract, furnishing the monthly
reports, analyzing the annual needs assessment data, and planning the schedule for the
coming contract year. EMT's contract covers expenses for staff, consultant trainer fees and
expenses, operational costs such as rent, utilities, telephone, photocopying, personal auto
mileage, supplies, and computer rental.

NOTICE OF INTENTION TO ADOPT A
CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE


NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Center for Management Development intends
to adopt a conflict of interest code pursuant to Government Code Section
87300. Pursuant to Government Code Section 87302, the code will designate
employees who must disclose certain investments, income, interests in real
property and business positions, and who must disqualify themselves from
making or participating in the making of governmental decisions affecting
those interests.

A written comment period has been established commencing on June 5, 1989
and terminating on July 21, 1989. Any interested person may submit written
comments concerning the proposed code no later than July 21, 1989, to the
Center for Management Development, 1000 Webster Street, Fairfield, CA 94533.
No public hearing on this matter will be held unless any interested person or
his or her representative requests, no later than 15 days prior to the close
of the written comment period, a public hearing.

The Center for Management Development has prepared a written explanation
of the reasons for the designations and the disclosure responsibilities and
has available all of the information upon which its proposal is based.

Copies of the proposed code and all of the information upon which it is
based may be obtained from the Center for Management Development, c/o City of
Fairfield, 1000 Webster Street, Fairfield, CA 94533. Any inquiries concerning
the proposed code should be directed to Yolanda Irigon (707) 428-7397.


Note: This notice should be filed with the Fair Political Practices
Commission and served individually on agency employees and officers affected
by this code 45 days prior to agency action.

2712

CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR THE

CENTER FOR MANAGEMENT DEVELOPMENT


The Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 81000, et seq.,

requires state and local government agencies to adopt and promulgate Conflict

of Interest Codes. The Fair Political Practices Commission has adopted a

regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730, which contains the terms of a

standard Conflict of Interest Code, which can be incorporated by reference,

and which may be amended by the Fair Political Practices Commission to conform

to amendments in the Political Reform Act after public notice and hearings.

Therefore, the terms of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730 and any amendments to

it duly adopted by the Fair Political Practices Commission are hereby

incorporated by reference and, along with the attached Appendix in which

officials and employees are designated and disclosure categories are set

forth, constitute the Conflict of Interest Code of the Center for Management

Development (CMD).

Pursuant to Section 4(A) of the Standard Code, designated employees shall

file statements of economic interests with their agency. Upon receipt of the

statements of the members of the Board of Directors, the agency shall make and

retain a copy and forward the original of these statements to the Fair

Political Practices Commission·Statements for all other designated employees

will be retained by the agency.

APPENDIX
TO
CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE

CENTER FOR MANAGEMENT DEVELOPMENT

Designated Employees                                   Disclosure Categories

Members of the Board of Directors                     All

Consultants                                           All

*  "Consultants shall include any natural person who provides, under
contract, information, advice recommendation or counsel to a state or
local government agency, provided, however, that "consultant" shall not
include a person who: (1) Conducts research and arrives at conclusions
with respect to his or her rendition of information, advice,
recommendation or counsel independent of the control and direction of the
agency or of any agency official, other than normal contract monitoring
and (B) Possesses no authority with respect to any agency decision beyond
the rendition of information, advice, recommendation or counsel.

The Chairperson may determine in writing that a particular consultant,
although a "Designated Employee", is hired to perform a range of duties
that are limited in scope and thus is not required to fully comply with
the disclosure requirements described in this section. Such written
determination shall include a description of the consultant's duties and,
based upon that description, a statement of the extent of disclosure
requirements. Such determination shall be retained with the agency.
Nothing herein excuses any such consultants from any other provisions of
the Conflict of Interest.

Disclosure Categories

Persons designated to report in any of the following categories shall
disclose relevant information concerning:

    (a)  Investments in;

    (b)  Sources of income from; or

    (c)  His or her status as a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee
         or any position of management in any business entities hereafter
         described.

Category 1.  Business entities which are of the type to supply to the CMD
materials, products, supplies, commodities or equipment utilized by the CMD.

Category 2.  Business entities which are of the type to supply to the CMD
services, including professional services, utilized by the CMD.

Category 3. Business entities engaged in the business of insurance including, but not limited to, insurance companies, carriers, holding companies, underwriters, brokers, solicitors, agents, adjusters, claims manager and actuaries.

Category 4. Financial institutions including, but not limited to, banks, savings and loans associations and credit unions which are located in, doing business in, or have done business in the jurisdiction of CMD.

Category 5. Business entities or persons who have filed a claim, or have a claim pending, against CMD or any member of CMD.

Category 6. All interests in real property located within the jurisdiction of the Fund.

(This is the last page of the conflict of interest code of the Center for Management Development)


CERTIFICATION OF FPPC APPROVAL

Pursuant to Government Code Section 87303, the conflict of interest code for the Center for Management Development was approved on ________________________ by the Executive Director of the Fair Political Practice Commission.

By: ________________________________
Effective: ________________________

EMT Associates, Inc.
Evaluation, Management and Training
3090 Fite Circle, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95827
(916) 363-9415

July 11, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Who Must File Statements of Economic Interests

Dear Sir/Madame:

EMT Associates, Inc., is a management consulting firm in Sacramento. Our clients are
government agencies such as the Department of Alcohol and Drug Programs, the California
Youth Authority, the Arizona Department of Corrections, federal agencies like the National
Institute of Justice, or local agencies such as the City Council of Sacramento. We engage
in competitive bid contracting for our work, or extensions thereof.

Recently the City of Fairfield requested EMT's President, Joel Phillips, complete a Form 730
to comply with reporting requirements stated therein. Upon review of the form and the
language identifying who must file a Form 730, EMT respectfully requests a ruling from the
Fair Political Practices Commission indicating we are neither the type of entity targeted by
the conflict of interest disclosure nor are we at risk of engaging in such activities.

The City of Fairfield has requested EMT's response with appropriate ruling from the Fair
Political Practices Commission. I enclose a copy of the latest correspondence from Yolanda
Irigon of City of Fairfield, a copy of Fairfield's Conflict of Interest Code, and a description
of EMT's contractual services pertaining to her letter.

As I understand the instructions, Form 730 is for (1) elected officials, or (2) designated
employees who make decisions which affect the financial interests of the state or local
government agency. We are neither.

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 11, 1990
Page 2




According to the definition of who must file, statements of economic interest:

WHO MUST FILE STATEMENTS OF ECONOMIC INTERESTS?

All state and local government agencies (including cities and counties) are required
to adopt conflict of interest codes designating certain positions which entail the
making or participation in the making of decision which may affect financial interests.
You must file a Statement of Economic Interest (Form 730) because you are an
employee or an appointed official whose position is designated in your agency's
conflict of interest code. Certain elected officials not specified in Gov. Code Section
87200 file form 730 if required by a conflict of interest code.

As a consultant to the Center for Management Develop, (CMD) EMT Associates is
designated in CMD's Conflict of Interest Code because we provide professional services to
CMD, under category 2 of disclosure categories. However, the specific professional services
are not defined in the code, and the

Chairperson may determine in writing that a particular consultant, although a
"Designated Employee", is hired to perform a range of duties that are limited in
scope and thus is not required to fully comply with the disclosure requirement
described in the section.

I maintain that EMT's duties are indeed, limited in scope and request that the Chairperson
to CMD grant us such an exception. The reasons for this request follow:

1. EMT Associates acts in behalf of CMD as a contractor to broker for training services
among up to 35 individual training consultants.

2. EMT is under contract to CMD for 10 months out of the year, at a level of effort
that is the equivalent of .25 FTE.

3. EMT's duties are narrowly confined to scheduling, logistics, and monthly reporting

4. EMT plays no role in decisions that effect financial interests of CMD or the City of
Fairfield. There is no opportunity for a conflict of interest to arise in the context of
EMT's contract with CMD.

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 11, 1990
Page 3




EMT's reluctance to simply complete the Form 730 and submit it as requested is a function
of two issues:

     1. Once we complete Form 730, are we likely to subject ourselves to similar
        requirements from all our California contracting agencies? We hope to avoid
        establishing an unnecessary precedent.

     2. The nature of our work and contractual relationships does not appear to fit either
        description of who must file. We would be more than willing to submit a Form 730
        if we understood the rationale for our case.

Please review the enclosed documents and direct your written finding to Yolanda Irigon with
the City of Fairfield, 1000 Webster Street, Fairfield, CA 94533-4883 and send a copy to me
or Joël at EMT. You may contact either Joël Phillips or me to discuss and resolve this
issue. Thank you in advance for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Lynne P. Cannady

LPC/ba

Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: EMT Associates, Inc. seeks a ruling from the FPPC clarifying whether its president must file Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) when the company contracts with government agencies like the City of Fairfield, arguing that EMT is neither an elected official nor a designated employee making decisions affecting the agency's financial interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter (primary) plus supporting materials including a conflict of interest code, notice of intent to adopt code, and service descriptions. The primary document is an incoming request from EMT Associates to FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No FPPC conclusion section found in document.

embedding.summary: This document is an incoming request to the FPPC from EMT Associates, Inc., seeking clarification on whether its president must file statements of economic interests (Form 730) under the City of Fairfield's conflict of interest code. The request includes background on EMT's consulting services, the Center for Management Development's conflict of interest code, and correspondence addressing the applicability of disclosure requirements to EMT as a contractor.
